name: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm ( AAA )
description: This test case evaluates identification of patients with abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) based on a complex phenotype algorithm that includes three case types: patients with AAA repair procedures (Case Type 1), patients with ruptured AAA diagnoses from vascular clinics (Case Type 2), and patients with multiple unruptured AAA diagnoses from vascular clinics (Case Type 3). The phenotype requires integration of procedure codes, condition codes, and clinical context including department specificity and encounter timing.
prompt: Find all patients with abdominal aortic aneurysm who have undergone repair procedures or have documented diagnoses of ruptured or unruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm.

required_codes.display: Endovascular repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm or dissection; using aorto-aortic tube prosthesis
Endovascular repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm or dissection; using modular bifurcated prosthesis (1 docking limb)
Endovascular repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm or dissection; using modular bifurcated prosthesis (2 docking limbs)
Endovascular repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm or dissection; using unibody bifurcated prosthesis
Endovascular repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm or dissection; using aorto-uniiliac or aorto-unifemoral prosthesis

Direct repair of aneurysm, pseudoaneurysm, or excision (partial or total) and graft insertion, with or without patch graft; for aneurysm, pseudoaneurysm, and associated occlusive disease, abdominal aorta
Direct repair of aneurysm, pseudoaneurysm, or excision (partial or total) and graft insertion, with or without patch graft; for ruptured aneurysm, abdominal aorta
